skill.name: pr-prepare
skill.description: Prepare pull request descriptions from local Git context using a project-focused template.
skill.summary: Creates structured pull request titles and descriptions from branch diffs, changed files, impact, and test notes.
skill.author: ByronWilliamsCPA

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command and cryptography patterns. Review found legitimate Git and local test command guidance for PR preparation, plus false positives from Markdown backticks and words like description. The skill is publishable with a warning because it asks assistants to read local repository history and diffs.

medium_findings.title: External Git Commands Read Repository Context
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs assistants to run git status, git log, git diff --stat, and git diff. These commands are expected for PR preparation, but they expose branch history and source diffs to the assistant.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly listed and semantically tied to the skill purpose. They are not malicious, but they intentionally read repository state and diffs.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Substitution in Git Examples
medium_findings.description: The git log and git diff examples use command substitution to calculate the merge base. The inputs are hardcoded Git references, so injection risk is low, but shell execution is still required.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command substitution is explicit in the file. The reviewed examples do not concatenate untrusted user input, which limits severity.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Many static hits are Markdown code fences or inline formatting, not Ruby execution. The backticks document commands and labels rather than executing code inside the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context shows Markdown examples and inline command text. No Ruby code or runtime backtick execution is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The high-risk weak cryptography alerts are false positives caused by natural language such as description and descriptions. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing call, or encryption code appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each cited line contains PR description language rather than crypto code. There is no evidence of DES, MD5, SHA1, or other weak algorithm usage.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Shell Commands in Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell commands for Git inspection and optional PR creation. This is useful for the task, but users should understand that the assistant may execute local repository commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and relevant to the workflow. The risk comes from local command execution, not from evidence of malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Pull request descriptions often miss context, impact, and testing details. This skill gathers local Git context and formats a clear PR title and description for review.
actual_capabilities: Runs local Git inspection commands to understand branch changes.
Summarizes changed components, purpose, impact, and testing status.
Formats PR descriptions with summary, changes, impact, testing, and notes sections.
Suggests a conventional commit style PR title.
Reminds users about CodeRabbit summary placeholders and GitHub CLI creation commands.
limitations: Requires a local Git repository with an accessible comparison branch.
Assumes main is the base branch unless the user or repository indicates otherwise.
Does not guarantee that tests or linting actually passed unless those commands were run.
Does not create or publish a pull request unless the user separately requests that action.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Feature PR
use_cases.description: Turn a completed feature branch into a reviewer-ready pull request description with impact and test notes.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team PRs
use_cases.description: Use the same structure for summaries, changed components, risk notes, and testing across a project.

use_cases.title: Document Review Context
use_cases.description: Capture changed files and branch history before asking reviewers to inspect a bug fix or refactor.

prompt_templates.title: Basic PR Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a ready-to-edit PR description.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a PR description for this branch using the project template.

prompt_templates.title: Base Branch Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Specify a base branch when the repository does not use main.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a PR description comparing this branch against develop. Include a suggested conventional commit title.

prompt_templates.title: Risk-Focused PR
prompt_templates.scenario: Ask for extra attention to impact and rollout concerns.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare the PR for this branch. Highlight breaking changes, migration steps, test coverage, and follow-up work.

prompt_templates.title: Reviewer-Optimized PR
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a concise review package.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the branch diff and write a concise PR title, description, impact section, testing checklist, and reviewer notes.

output_examples.input: Prepare the PR for this authentication branch.
output_examples.output: Suggested title: feat: add OAuth sign-in. The response includes a summary, changed authentication components, user impact, completed validation, and follow-up notes.

output_examples.input: Write a PR description for a bug fix against develop.
output_examples.output: The skill compares the branch to develop and produces a focused description with the bug cause, fix summary, affected files, and testing checklist.

output_examples.input: Draft a PR for a refactor with no behavior changes.
output_examples.output: The result explains the refactor scope, states that behavior is unchanged, lists validation performed, and suggests a refactor-style title.

best_practices: Confirm the intended base branch before generating the final description.
Run relevant tests and linting before marking checklist items as complete.
Review the generated impact and notes sections for project-specific context.
anti_patterns: Do not mark tests as passing unless they were actually run.
Do not include sensitive diff details in public PRs without review.
Do not use the generated title without checking team naming rules.

faq.question: Does this skill create the pull request automatically?
faq.answer: No. It prepares the title and description. It only suggests GitHub CLI commands for the user to run if appropriate.

faq.question: Which Git commands does it use?
faq.answer: It uses local Git status, log, diff stat, and diff commands to understand branch changes.

faq.question: Can I use a base branch other than main?
faq.answer: Yes. Tell the assistant the base branch, or adjust the comparison command before generating the description.

faq.question: Will it verify tests?
faq.answer: It can report test commands and checklist status, but results are reliable only when the commands are run.

faq.question: Is repository content sent outside my environment?
faq.answer: The skill itself defines local commands only. Your AI tool may process the command output according to its own data handling rules.

faq.question: Can it follow my project template?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the template or keep one in the repository so the assistant can format the PR consistently.
